name: Apple Upside-Down Cake
recipeInstructions:
1. Preheat oven to 375°F.
2. Melt 1/4 cup butter in an 8-inch square baking pan. Add brown sugar and spices; mix well.
3. Peel apples and remove cores, discarding them. Cut apples into rings.
4. Arrange apple rings over the brown sugar mixture in the bottom of the pan; sprinkle with lemon juice.
5. In a large bowl, combine dry ingredients. Cut remaining 3 tablespoons butter until the mixture resembles coarse crumbs.
6. Add milk and vanilla to the dry ingredients; beat at low speed until the dry ingredients are moistened.
7. Continue beating for 2 minutes at medium speed. Blend in the egg yolk.
8. Beat egg whites with a mixer until stiff peaks form. Gently fold into the batter.
9. Pour batter over the apples in the pan.
10. Bake for 35 minutes or until a wooden pick inserted in the center comes out clean.
11. Cool in the pan on a wire rack for 5 minutes. Loosen edges and invert onto a serving plate. Let stand 1 minute before removing the pan. Serve warm.
